title: Delta pilots open voting for strike authorization as contract negotiations falter
text: Delta pilots opened the vote to decided whether to strike over contract negations over compensation for flight cancellations, competitive pay, and hours.

title: Delta Air Lines: Bolstering Its Balance Sheet
text: The company sees corporate travel strengthening as global business travel spending is expected to gain 33.8% in 2022. Delta is targeting to reduce its debt by $5 billion by 2024, which is a 25% reduction in net debt.

title: Why Are Airline Stocks Down Today?
text: Airline stocks are in decline today due to the storm sweeping across the American south. Hurricane Ian made landfall in Florida yesterday, and could make landfall again soon outside of Charleston, South Carolina.

title: After 10% Drop Last Week, Can Delta Air Lines Stock Bounce?
text: Della Air Lines Inc. stock (NYSE: DAL) is down 10% in the past week, strongly underperforming the S&P 500 which was down 4% over this period.

title: Why Airline Stocks Are Falling Again Today
text: The industry could struggle to power through the latest headwinds.

title: Why Airline Stocks Are Losing Altitude Today
text: Macroeconomic conditions are making it hard for this sector to take flight.

title: 3 Airline Stocks to Watch Amid Air-Travel Demand Strength
text: Key players in the Zacks Transportation -- Airline industry like Delta (DAL), United Airlines (UAL) and JetBlue (JBLU) are likely to benefit immensely from increased passenger volumes.

title: What The U.S. Airline Industry Could Look Like In 2035
text: The airline industry in always changing. By 2035, there will likely be fewer airlines, more airport automation, more efficient fleets, and lower fares for consumers.

title: 3 Large-Cap Transportation Stocks in View Despite Market Ruckus
text: Investors should tap transportation stocks like Old Dominion (ODFL), Delta (DAL) and Expeditors (EXPD) for handsome returns.

title: Delta Air Lines And Alaska Airlines: 2 Speculative Buys In A Sea Of Airline Stock Desperation
text: As the cost of living increases, travelers might turn more cost-conscious again but legacy carriers do offer opportunity. Opportunity arises in business travel as well as long-haul flights.

title: EXCLUSIVE Delta Air Lines readies refinery to process biofuels -sources
text: Delta Air Lines Inc plans to start receiving shipments of renewable fuel feedstocks at its Trainer, Pennsylvania, refinery as part of a strategy that could reduce its environmental liabilities by hundreds of millions of dollars, according to three sources familiar with the matter.

title: Delta Air Lines, Inc. (DAL) Morgan Stanley 10th Annual Laguna Conference (Transcript)

title: Why Airline Stocks Are Falling Today
text: Economic uncertainty is making it hard for this sector to take off.

title: CEO Ed Bastian Is Selling Delta (DAL) Stock as Air Travel Surges
text: Delta Airlines (NYSE: DAL ) is in the spotlight following a $1.5 million sale by CEO Ed Bastian. The sale of DAL stock comes as air travel over the Labor Day Weekend surpassed air travel volume during the 2019 LDW.

title: U.S. must do more to block harmful airline mergers, anti-monopoly group says
text: The U.S. Transportation Department (USDOT) must step up efforts to stop anti-competitive U.S. airline mergers and hold carriers accountable for flight cancellations and poor customer service, an influential Washington-based think-thank said on Wednesday.

title: Here's why airline stocks are taking off this week
text: Airline stocks outperformed the broader equity market on Wednesday, getting a boost from a rosier outlook for United Airlines Holdings Inc. and news that U.S. airports recently welcomed more travelers than they had before the pandemic.

title: Holiday air travel tops pre-pandemic levels for the 1st time
text: For the first time since COVID-19 brought air travel to a standstill, the number of people streaming through U.S. airport security checkpoints over a holiday weekend exceeded pre-pandemic levels.

title: Delta Air Lines to Present at the Morgan Stanley Laguna Conference
text: ATLANTA , Sept. 7, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) will present at the Morgan Stanley Laguna Conference at 10:35 a.m.

title: Delta (DAL) Inks Environment-Friendly Deal With DG Fuels
text: DG Fuels will supply Delta ((AL) with 385 million gallons of sustainable aviation fuel from 2027-end.

title: Labor Day weekend air travel surpasses 2019 levels as airlines cap a rocky summer
text: It was the first holiday weekend since the pandemic began that TSA screenings topped 2019 tallies, a milestone in air travel's bumpy recovery.

title: Airlines count on business travelers to keep recovery going
text: With summer vacations winding down, airlines are counting on the return of more business travelers to keep their pandemic recovery going into the fall.

title: Delta Air Lines To Thrive With New Consumer Protections
text: Delta has historically provided some of the best service among U.S. airlines. The U.S. Dept. of Transportation is enhancing air traveler protections.

title: What Awaits U.S. Airlines This Labor Day Travel Period?
text: United Airlines (UAL), American Airlines (AAL) and Delta (DAL) expect to carry 2.6 million, 2.5 million and 2.9 million passengers, respectively, during the Labor Day holiday period.

title: Delta Air Lines has refunded more than 11 million tickets worth a total of $6 billion since the start of 2020
text: Delta Air Lines CEO Ed Bastian added that 20% of these refunds were made in 2022, which has been a particularly tumultuous year for air travel.
